title: U.S., UK move to align rules for tokenized finance across world's largest financial markets
content: The joint roadmap aims to reduce regulatory friction for tokenized assets while strengthening ties between the world's two largest financial centers.
summary: The U.S. and UK are collaborating to align regulations for tokenized finance, aiming to reduce friction for tokenized assets. This joint roadmap seeks to strengthen ties between the world's two largest financial centers and streamline the adoption of digital assets in traditional finance.

title: Bitcoin tops $64,000 as cooling U.S. inflation guts the Fed rate-hike trade
content: The June CPI print  pulled hike odds from 43% to 13%, with analysts now watching the September FOMC meeting for further cues on positioning.
summary: Bitcoin's price surpassed $64,000 after cooling U.S. inflation data significantly reduced the odds of further Fed rate hikes. The June CPI report caused hike probabilities to drop from 43% to 13%, shifting analysts' focus to the September FOMC meeting for future market direction.

title: Wikipedia blackout could hurt how AI engines like ChatGPT understand crypto
content: Wikipedia observes guidelines on crypto coverage that date back many years and may now be outdated, according to the report.
summary: A potential Wikipedia blackout could negatively impact how AI models like ChatGPT comprehend cryptocurrency. This is because Wikipedia's long-standing crypto coverage guidelines are reportedly outdated, raising concerns about the quality of data available to AI for learning about the sector.

title: JPMorgan says Hyperliquid's rise threatens Circle's USDC economics
content: Hyperliquid's deal with Circle and Coinbase creates a "prisoner's dilemma" that puts pressure on earnings from the dollar-pegged stablecoin.
summary: JPMorgan warns that Hyperliquid's rise poses a threat to Circle's USDC economics due to a deal with Circle and Coinbase, creating a prisoner's dilemma that pressures earnings from the dollar-pegged stablecoin. This could impact the stability of USDC. The deal may force Circle to reevaluate its business model.

title: Ethereum Foundation spinout EthSystems targets banks with blockchain privacy technology
content: The spinout comes amid one of the biggest organizational shakeups at the Ethereum Foundation in years.
summary: The Ethereum Foundation has spun out a new company called EthSystems, which aims to provide blockchain privacy technology to banks. This move comes during a significant organizational shakeup at the Ethereum Foundation. The new company's focus is on bringing blockchain solutions to traditional financial institutions.

title: Hut 8 price target hiked to $165 at Benchmark as AI pivot reshapes valuation
content: Mark Palmer said Hut 8's Beacon Point project transforms the company into an AI infrastructure platform with a long growth runway.
summary: Hut 8's price target has been increased to $165 by Benchmark due to its pivot into AI infrastructure, specifically the Beacon Point project. This shift is expected to transform the company and provide a long growth runway. The project has significant implications for Hut 8's valuation.

title: ECB picks firms including Deutsche Bank, Revolut for digital euro pilot
content: The pilot will test a beta version of the digital euro for online, offline, in-store, and e-commerce payments with ECB and central bank staff as users.
summary: The European Central Bank has chosen firms including Deutsche Bank and Revolut to participate in a digital euro pilot, testing online and offline payments. The pilot will use ECB and central bank staff as users. The goal is to test a beta version of the digital euro for various payment scenarios.

title: Japan’s biggest card network taps Circle to bring stablecoins to 40 million merchants
content: JCB and Circle will explore using USDC for cross-border payments and merchant transactions as Japan ramps up efforts to bring stablecoins into everyday commerce.
summary: Japan's largest card network, JCB, has partnered with Circle to introduce stablecoins to 40 million merchants, exploring the use of USDC for cross-border payments and transactions. This move is part of Japan's efforts to integrate stablecoins into everyday commerce. The partnership aims to facilitate smoother transactions and expand the reach of stablecoins in the country.

title: U.S.-Iran escalation weighs on bitcoin, stocks as oil climbs
content: Your day-ahead look for July 14, 2026
summary: The U.S.-Iran escalation is affecting the prices of bitcoin and stocks, with oil prices climbing. This development has significant implications for the global economy and financial markets. The situation is being closely monitored by investors and traders.

title: Bitcoin's BIP-110 sparked a fight over who gets to decide the future of Bitcoin
content: BIP-110 sought to limit non-financial data on Bitcoin's blockchain but instead reignited debates over censorship and decentralization.
summary: A proposed Bitcoin Improvement Proposal (BIP-110) aimed to limit non-financial data on Bitcoin's blockchain, but it sparked a debate over censorship and decentralization instead. The proposal reignited discussions about the future of Bitcoin and who should have control over its development. This has significant implications for the Bitcoin community.

title: Prediction markets just crushed traditional sportsbooks in a massive $50 billion World Cup breakout
content: Prediction markets just topped $50 billion in monthly volume, capping off the first month of the 2026 FIFA World Cup.
summary: Prediction markets exceeded $50 billion in monthly volume during the first month of the 2026 FIFA World Cup, surpassing traditional sportsbooks. This significant milestone marks a major breakthrough for prediction markets. The large volume indicates a growing interest in alternative betting platforms.

title: Bitcoin’s great rotation: Long-term holders pass supply to a new generation of buyers
content: Long-term holders are quietly transferring supply to a new generation of buyers, but looming Federal Reserve rate hikes could still trigger the capitulation markets have been waiting for.
summary: Long-term Bitcoin holders are transferring their supply to new buyers, but upcoming Federal Reserve rate hikes may trigger market capitulation. This shift in ownership could impact the cryptocurrency's future. The change is significant as it may signal a new era for Bitcoin.

title: Bitcoin steadies at $62,600 as South Koreans flee stocks rout for crypto
content: Bitcoin consolidated after Monday's selloff as Donald Trump threatened more Iran strikes, gold extended its slide and a KOSPI index meltdown sent South Korean crypto volumes soaring.
summary: Bitcoin stabilized at $62,600 after a Monday selloff, amid geopolitical tensions and a slide in gold. A notable meltdown in South Korea's KOSPI index led to a significant increase in crypto trading volumes as investors sought alternatives to traditional stocks.

title: Live updates: Bitcoin rises past $64,000 after soft inflation data, Warsh testimony
content: Trump reinstated the Hormuz blockade, sending oil higher, reversing the peace trade that helped bitcoin recover in early July.
summary: The Iran conflict has reignited, causing oil prices to rise and affecting Bitcoin's price, which is currently holding at $62,600. The reinstatement of the Hormuz blockade by Trump has increased rate-hike bets. The upcoming inflation print is expected to be the next test for Bitcoin's price.

title: U.S. government moves $288 million in seized bitcoin, ether to Coinbase Prime
content: Coins from the Farace and BTC-e seizures moved through fresh wallets before landing on the exchange, despite president Donald Trump's previous no-sell reserve order.
summary: The US government has moved $288 million in seized bitcoin and ether to Coinbase Prime, despite a previous order to reserve sales. The coins originated from the Farace and BTC-e seizures and were transferred through new wallets before landing on the exchange. This move may have implications for the crypto market.

title: Solo bitcoin miner makes $200,000 using $150 equipment
content: Solo Bitcoin mining has surged, with 24 blocks found in the past 12 months, a 41% increase year over year.
summary: A solo Bitcoin miner recently achieved a significant payout of $200,000 using only $150 worth of equipment. This event comes amidst a notable surge in solo Bitcoin mining, which has seen a 41% year-over-year increase, resulting in 24 blocks being found by solo miners in the last 12 months.

title: Bitcoin slips as traders lift July Fed rate hike bets ahead of Inflation report
content: Major cryptocurrencies have dropped by 2% or more in 24 hours as traders boosted bets of a July Fed rate hike.
summary: Bitcoin and other major cryptocurrencies have dropped in value as traders anticipate a potential July Fed rate hike, with many cryptocurrencies losing 2% or more in 24 hours. This shift is likely due to increased bets on a rate hike ahead of an upcoming inflation report. The cryptocurrency market is reacting to expected changes in monetary policy.

title: US freezes $131M in Iran-linked crypto as Middle East tensions rise
content: “US Treasury is committed to disrupting and degrading Iran’s illicit financial activities, including its abuse of digital assets,” US Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said Tuesday.
summary: The US Treasury has frozen $131M in Iran-linked crypto assets, aiming to disrupt Iran's illicit financial activities. This move comes as Middle East tensions rise. The action targets Iran's abuse of digital assets.

title: Over 95% of Coinbase’s code is now written with help of AI
content: Coinbase’s Rob Witoff said the company is leaning more on AI to help with execution, but still needs high-agency humans for judgment and strategy.
summary: Coinbase has revealed that over 95% of its code is now written with the help of artificial intelligence, with the company relying on AI for execution but still requiring human judgment and strategy. This shift highlights the growing role of AI in coding and development. The use of AI is expected to increase efficiency and speed up the coding process.

title: Three US senators oppose CLARITY Act on ethics grounds with vote expected soon
content: Senate Majority Leader John Thune reportedly said the chamber will consider a vote on the crypto market structure bill before Aug. 10, though it’s unclear whether it has enough Democratic support to pass.
summary: Three US senators are opposing the CLARITY Act, a crypto market structure bill, on ethics grounds. A vote on this significant bill is expected in the Senate before August 10, though its passage is uncertain due to unclear Democratic support.

title: Velocity raises $38M to build stablecoin treasury infrastructure for enterprises
content: Backed by Dragonfly, FirstMark and Coinbase Ventures, the startup is expanding software that helps businesses integrate stablecoins into treasury and payment workflows.
summary: Velocity has raised $38M from investors including Dragonfly and Coinbase Ventures to build stablecoin treasury infrastructure for enterprises. The startup aims to expand software that helps businesses integrate stablecoins into their treasury and payment workflows.

title: US, UK treasuries to align transatlantic rules on tokenization and stablecoins
content: The two governments issued recommendations on the treatment of digital assets as the US prepares to implement a 2025 law on payment stablecoins.
summary: The US and UK treasuries are collaborating to align transatlantic rules concerning tokenization and stablecoins. This initiative includes issuing recommendations on the treatment of digital assets as the US moves towards implementing a 2025 law specifically for payment stablecoins.

title: UK government defers capital gains on certain crypto with ‘no gain, no loss’ approach
content: The change in tax policy, expected to effect about 700,000 people in the UK, is tied to the disposal of crypto in lending and liquidity pools.
summary: The UK government has introduced a new tax policy that defers capital gains on certain crypto transactions, applying a 'no gain, no loss' approach to the disposal of crypto in lending and liquidity pools, affecting around 700,000 people. This change aims to simplify tax calculations for individuals involved in crypto lending and liquidity provision. The policy is expected to have a significant impact on the UK's crypto community.

title: JCB signs Circle MOU to test stablecoin payments in Japan
content: The companies will explore using USDC for JCB’s cross-border treasury operations and merchant payments in Japan as regulated stablecoin adoption gains momentum.
summary: JCB and Circle have signed an MOU to test stablecoin payments in Japan, exploring the use of USDC for cross-border treasury operations and merchant payments. This partnership aims to leverage regulated stablecoin adoption, which is gaining momentum. The collaboration will facilitate payments in Japan using USDC.

title: CleanSpark shares jump 22% after $6.6B Georgia data center lease
content: The Bitcoin miner’s Georgia agreement with an undisclosed investment-grade technology company expands its footprint in AI and high-performance computing infrastructure.
summary: CleanSpark, a Bitcoin miner, has signed a $6.6B lease for a data center in Georgia, expanding its presence in AI and high-performance computing infrastructure, causing its shares to jump 22%. The agreement is with an undisclosed investment-grade technology company. This deal is expected to grow CleanSpark's footprint in the industry.

title: Bitcoin jumps on lowest US CPI since 2020 as traders stay wary of $64K failure
content: BTC price action returned to $64,000 on low US CPI inflation but traders stayed wary of rejection at key resistance.
summary: Bitcoin's price has jumped to $64,000 following the release of the lowest US Consumer Price Index (CPI) since 2020, but traders are cautious due to potential rejection at this key resistance level. The low CPI indicates reduced inflation, which may have contributed to the price increase. However, the uncertainty surrounding the sustainability of this price movement persists.

title: OpenAI quietly adds Kalshi World Cup odds to ChatGPT: Report
content: OpenAI’s ChatGPT now displays Kalshi’s World Cup prediction market odds in search results, marking the AI firm’s first reported deal with a prediction market platform.
summary: OpenAI's ChatGPT now displays Kalshi's World Cup prediction market odds, marking the company's first deal with a prediction market platform. This integration aims to provide users with more information and insights. The deal is significant for both AI and crypto communities.

title: Strategy became a symbol of the dot-com crash: Could history repeat?
content: MicroStrategy blew up during the dot-com era, before Michael Saylor transformed it into the world's largest corporate Bitcoin holder. Did he learn his lesson?
summary: MicroStrategy, a company that previously failed during the dot-com era, has been transformed by Michael Saylor into the world's largest corporate Bitcoin holder, raising questions about whether history will repeat itself. The company's past experiences may have taught Saylor valuable lessons. MicroStrategy's current situation is being watched closely by investors and analysts.

title: Humanity Protocol to prioritize operational security following $36M hack
content: Humanity Protocol’s founder said they will refocus on operational security, as malicious actors are switching from smart contract vulnerabilities to exploiting human behavior.
summary: Humanity Protocol's founder announced a focus on operational security after the platform was hacked for $36M, with attackers now targeting human behavior rather than smart contract vulnerabilities. The hack highlights a shift in tactics by malicious actors. This change in approach necessitates a stronger emphasis on security measures.

title: ECB picks 36 payment providers to test digital euro ahead of 2027 pilot
content: The European Central Bank will beta test a digital euro with 36 providers, including Revolut, after the project drew more than 50 applications.
summary: The European Central Bank has selected 36 payment providers, including Revolut, to beta test a digital euro. The project received over 50 applications and is set to pilot in 2027. This development marks a significant step towards the potential adoption of a digital euro.

title: AI has not triggered DeFi ‘hackpocalypse,’ Dragonfly partner says
content: Total value stolen and median hack size are declining compared to 2025, signaling that the AI hacking apocalypse was a false alarm, argued Dragonfly managing partner Haseeb Qureshi.
summary: Dragonfly managing partner Haseeb Qureshi argues that the AI hacking apocalypse in DeFi is a false alarm, as the total value stolen and median hack size are declining compared to 2025. This contradicts the notion that AI has led to a significant increase in hacking incidents. The data suggests a more stable security landscape in DeFi.

title: South Korea to test tokenized government bonds with CBDC in 2027
content: South Korea will test tokenized government bonds linked to the Bank of Korea’s wholesale CBDC system in 2027 as token securities rules take effect.
summary: South Korea plans to pilot tokenized government bonds integrated with the Bank of Korea's wholesale Central Bank Digital Currency (CBDC) system in 2027. This initiative will coincide with the implementation of new token securities regulations.

title: US spot Bitcoin ETFs post $425M outflow after brief rebound
content: US spot Bitcoin ETFs saw their largest single-day outflows in July, with $424.66 million leaving the funds, reversing a brief return to positive weekly flows.
summary: US spot Bitcoin ETFs recorded their largest single-day outflow in July, with $424.66 million exiting the funds. This significant capital movement reverses a recent brief period of positive weekly flows.

title: US government moves $297M in seized Bitcoin, Ether to Coinbase Prime
content: The transfers have raised questions over Trump’s Bitcoin reserve pledge, though deposits to Coinbase Prime do not confirm an impending sale.
summary: The US government has transferred $297 million in seized Bitcoin and Ether to Coinbase Prime, sparking speculation about a potential sale, although deposits do not necessarily confirm this. The move has also raised questions about Trump's Bitcoin reserve pledge. The transfer is a significant development in the crypto space.

title: Bitcoin Covenants Part 4: OP_CHECKSIGFROMSTACK and OP_CAT
content: OP_CSFS authenticates signatures over arbitrary data. OP_CAT concatenates stack values into a single message. Together, they let Bitcoin scripts verify transaction structure at spend time, without pre-signed key management or new consensus rules beyond the two opcodes themselves.
summary: Bitcoin's OP_CHECKSIGFROMSTACK and OP_CAT enable scripts to verify transaction structures at spend time without pre-signed keys or new consensus rules. This is achieved through authenticating signatures over arbitrary data and concatenating stack values. The two opcodes enhance Bitcoin's scripting capabilities.

title: Global law firm launches MiCA compliance tool as crypto companies navigate new EU rules
content: Reed Smith’s Aquarius platform automates regulatory filings and legal workflows as demand for MiCA compliance tools grows across Europe.
summary: Global law firm Reed Smith has launched a compliance tool called Aquarius to help crypto companies navigate new EU rules under the Markets in Crypto-Assets (MiCA) regulation. The tool automates regulatory filings and legal workflows. This launch comes as demand for MiCA compliance tools grows across Europe.

title: Bolivia mulls recognizing USDT as payment currency amid dollar shortage
content: As foreign currency reserves remain under pressure, Bolivia is considering a framework to let the world’s largest stablecoin be used for payments, savings and trade.
summary: Bolivia is exploring a framework to officially recognize USDT, the world's largest stablecoin, for payments, savings, and trade. This consideration comes as the country faces ongoing pressure on its foreign currency reserves and aims to address a dollar shortage.

title: How Asset Managers Are Investing in Crypto
content: Asset managers invest in crypto mainly through regulated products: spot ETFs, digital asset funds, tokenized funds, qualified custodians, and blockchain company equity. Most invest on behalf of clients rather than for their own accounts. Their aim is to deliver digital asset exposure inside the brokerage, retirement, and advisory structures clients already use, with the custody, […]
summary: Asset managers are investing in cryptocurrency through regulated products such as spot ETFs and digital asset funds, primarily on behalf of their clients. The goal is to provide digital asset exposure within existing brokerage and retirement structures. This approach allows for custody and other services to be integrated into clients' existing accounts.

title: Bitcoin vs Gold as a Reserve Asset
content: A reserve asset is something an institution or government holds to preserve value, provide liquidity, and act as a store of wealth outside its day-to-day operating currency. Bitcoin and gold are both treated as reserve assets because each is scarce, hard to counterfeit, and independent of any single company’s performance. Gold has filled that role […]
summary: Bitcoin and gold are being considered as reserve assets due to their scarcity and independence, with institutions and governments holding them to preserve value and provide liquidity. Both assets are hard to counterfeit and are not tied to any single company's performance. The comparison between the two highlights their potential as stores of wealth.

title: Ark Invest adds $14 million in Circle shares while selling Robinhood
content: Ark Invest bought $14 million worth of Circle and $1.5 million worth of Block Inc., while selling $3.2 million worth of Robinhood.
summary: Ark Invest has purchased $14 million in Circle shares and $1.5 million in Block Inc. shares, while selling $3.2 million worth of Robinhood. This move indicates a strategic investment in companies related to cryptocurrency and blockchain. The investment in Circle, a firm behind the USDC stablecoin, is particularly noteworthy.

title: Ethereum Foundation privacy team spins out as for-profit EthSystems to serve institutions with Lubin, Bitmine backing
content: The startup will build tech and provide consulting to help institutions operate on Ethereum while maintaining confidentiality.
summary: The Ethereum Foundation's privacy team is spinning out to form EthSystems, a new for-profit startup. Backed by figures like Joseph Lubin and Bitmine, EthSystems will provide technology and consulting services to help institutions operate on the Ethereum blockchain while ensuring confidentiality.

title: US-UK transatlantic taskforce unveils digital asset roadmap promoting stablecoin innovation
content: The U.S. and UK are deepening cooperation on digital assets, particularly for stablecoins and tokenized assets.
summary: The US and UK have formed a transatlantic taskforce to promote digital asset innovation, focusing on stablecoins and tokenized assets. This cooperation aims to deepen their collaboration on digital assets. The taskforce has unveiled a roadmap for stablecoin innovation.

title: Wall Street turns cautious on Circle as analysts warn USDC economics face mounting pressure
content: Mizuho downgraded the stock and JPMorgan lowered earnings estimates, citing concerns that USDC is coming under pressure.
summary: Wall Street analysts have downgraded Circle's stock and lowered earnings estimates due to concerns about the stability of USDC. This comes as USDC faces mounting pressure, sparking caution among investors. The downgrade and lowered estimates may impact Circle's financial performance.

title: ‘Soft print, hard regime’: Bitcoin climbs toward $64,000 as June CPI falls 0.4% in largest monthly drop since 2020
content: June CPI fell 0.4% and core held at 2.6% annually, lifting BTC toward $64,000 as analysts flip from fears to a summer recovery case.
summary: Bitcoin's price rises towards $64,000 as June's Consumer Price Index (CPI) sees a drop of 0.4%, the largest monthly decrease since 2020. This change has led analysts to shift from fearing a downturn to anticipating a summer recovery. The core CPI remains steady at 2.6% annually.

title: Benchmark sees ‘long pathway for growth’ in Hut 8, raises target to $165 on $16.8 billion AI data center deals
content: Benchmark raised Hut 8's target to $165, citing $16.8 billion in AI data center contracts and Beacon Point commercialization.
summary: Benchmark has raised Hut 8's target to $165 due to the company's $16.8 billion AI data center contracts and the commercialization of Beacon Point. This move indicates a positive outlook for Hut 8's growth. The large value of the contracts suggests a significant opportunity for the company.

title: Dragonfly, FirstMark lead $38 million Series A for stablecoin startup Velocity with support from Coinbase, Ripple and more
content: The startup enables corporate users to use stablecoins while connecting to traditional banking rails and compliance systems.
summary: Velocity, a startup that enables corporate users to utilize stablecoins while connecting to traditional banking systems, has secured $38 million in Series A funding led by Dragonfly and FirstMark, with support from notable companies like Coinbase and Ripple. This funding round highlights the growing interest in stablecoin adoption and integration with traditional finance. The investment will likely facilitate further development of Velocity's technology.

title: Decentralized AI project Nous Research in talks to raise $75M at $1.5B valuation: report
content: Nous Research is raising at least $75 million led by Robot Ventures at a $1.5 billion valuation, per TechCrunch.
summary: Nous Research, a decentralized AI project, is in talks to raise $75 million at a $1.5 billion valuation, led by Robot Ventures. The project aims to advance AI technology in a decentralized manner. This investment could significantly impact the AI and crypto communities.

title: European Central Bank taps 36 payment providers for yearlong digital euro pilot
content: The ECB selected 36 payment providers to test a beta digital euro in a yearlong pilot beginning in late 2027.
summary: The European Central Bank has selected 36 payment providers to participate in a yearlong pilot for a digital euro, set to begin in late 2027. This pilot aims to test a beta version of the digital euro. The move marks a significant step towards the potential adoption of a central bank-issued digital currency in Europe.

title: Distributed compute startup Boundless expands 4,000-GPU network from ZK to AI
content: Boundless, which built a 4,000 GPU cluster to settle Ethereum and Base ZK proofs on Bitcoin, is expanding to AI compute.
summary: Boundless, a distributed compute startup, is expanding its 4,000-GPU network from zero-knowledge proofs to artificial intelligence compute, having previously used it to settle Ethereum and Base ZK proofs on Bitcoin. This expansion marks a new direction for the company, leveraging its existing infrastructure for AI applications. The move demonstrates the versatility of Boundless' technology

title: China’s Prosecutors Move to Treat Crypto Mixers as Evidence of Money Laundering
content:
Bitcoin Magazine

China’s Prosecutors Move to Treat Crypto Mixers as Evidence of Money Laundering
China's Supreme People's Procuratorate published proposals that would make it easier to prosecute crypto-related money laundering, including treating the use of mixers and privacy coins as presumptive evidence of laundering intent.
This post China’s Prosecutors Move to Treat Crypto Mixers as Evidence of Money Laundering first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Micah Zimmerman.
summary: China's Supreme People's Procuratorate has proposed new guidelines that would consider the use of cryptocurrency mixers and privacy coins as presumptive evidence of money laundering intent. These proposals aim to simplify the prosecution of crypto-related money laundering cases within the country.

title: The Bitcoin Softfork That Tried to Police “Junk Data” — And Why It’s Already Failing
content:
Bitcoin Magazine

The Bitcoin Softfork That Tried to Police “Junk Data” — And Why It’s Already Failing
Bitcoin’s real power is that anyone willing to pay fees can write to the ledger. BIP-110’s backers want to change that for data-heavy transactions. The backlash reveals what’s truly at stake.
This post The Bitcoin Softfork That Tried to Police “Junk Data” — And Why It’s Already Failing first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Brandon Black.
summary: A proposed Bitcoin softfork, BIP-110, aimed to restrict data-heavy transactions by policing 'junk data', but it's already facing backlash. The softfork's failure highlights the tension between controlling transaction data and preserving Bitcoin's openness. The proposal's backers want to change Bitcoin's ledger writing rules, but opponents argue it undermines the network's core principles.

title: CleanSpark Signs $6.6 Billion Data Center Lease as Bitcoin Miner Pivots to Compute
content:
Bitcoin Magazine

CleanSpark Signs $6.6 Billion Data Center Lease as Bitcoin Miner Pivots to Compute
CleanSpark, the Nasdaq-listed bitcoin miner, said on July 14 that it has signed a 20-year infrastructure lease with an unnamed high-investment-grade global technology company at its campus in Sandersville, Georgia.  The deal marks the firm’s largest step from pure bitcoin mining toward high-performance computing for hyperscale clients. The lease covers data center infrastructure that will […]
This post CleanSpark Signs $6.6 Billion Data Center Lease as Bitcoin Miner Pivots to Compute first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Micah Zimmerman.
summary: CleanSpark, a Nasdaq-listed bitcoin miner, has signed a 20-year infrastructure lease for $6.6 billion to expand its operations into high-performance computing. The lease marks a significant shift for the company from pure bitcoin mining to serving hyperscale clients. This move is part of CleanSpark's efforts to diversify its services.

title: UK Adopts ‘No Gain, No Loss’ Tax Treatment for Crypto Lending and Liquidity Pools
content:
Bitcoin Magazine

UK Adopts ‘No Gain, No Loss’ Tax Treatment for Crypto Lending and Liquidity Pools
The UK will introduce a "no gain, no loss" tax treatment for qualifying crypto lending and DeFi liquidity pool transactions from April 6, 2027, deferring Capital Gains Tax until users make an economic disposal of their cryptocurrency.
This post UK Adopts ‘No Gain, No Loss’ Tax Treatment for Crypto Lending and Liquidity Pools first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Micah Zimmerman.
summary: The UK will introduce a 'no gain, no loss' tax treatment for crypto lending and liquidity pool transactions, deferring Capital Gains Tax until users make an economic disposal of their cryptocurrency, starting April 6, 2027. This new tax treatment aims to simplify the tax process for crypto users. The change will affect qualifying crypto lending and DeFi liquidity pool transactions.

title: Bitcoin Price Jumps Above $64,000 as Cooler-Than-Expected Inflation Strengthens the Case for Rate Cuts
content:
Bitcoin Magazine

Bitcoin Price Jumps Above $64,000 as Cooler-Than-Expected Inflation Strengthens the Case for Rate Cuts
Bitcoin price climbed above $64,000 after cooler-than-expected June inflation boosted expectations for Federal Reserve rate cuts, though sticky core inflation and rising oil prices continue to cloud the outlook.
This post Bitcoin Price Jumps Above $64,000 as Cooler-Than-Expected Inflation Strengthens the Case for Rate Cuts first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Micah Zimmerman.
summary: The Bitcoin price has jumped above $64,000 due to cooler-than-expected June inflation, which has strengthened the case for Federal Reserve rate cuts. However, core inflation and rising oil prices may still impact the outlook. The price increase is a significant development in the cryptocurrency market.

title: Bitwise Sees a Bottom in Bitcoin’s Worst Vibes Yet: ‘Darkest Before the Dawn’
content:
Bitcoin Magazine

Bitwise Sees a Bottom in Bitcoin’s Worst Vibes Yet: ‘Darkest Before the Dawn’ 
Bitwise says Bitcoin's brutal nine-month downturn masks a stronger-than-ever foundation, arguing institutional adoption, corporate buying, and improving market infrastructure position the industry for a rebound despite record ETF outflows and weak sentiment.
This post Bitwise Sees a Bottom in Bitcoin’s Worst Vibes Yet: ‘Darkest Before the Dawn’  first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Micah Zimmerman.
summary: Bitwise believes that despite Bitcoin's 9-month downturn, the foundation for the industry is stronger than ever due to institutional adoption and improving market infrastructure, positioning it for a rebound. The company argues that the current weak sentiment masks a positive outlook. Record ETF outflows have occurred during this time.

title: ‘Don’t Let China Win’: President Trump Presses Senate on Clarity Act in Final Stretch
content:
Bitcoin Magazine

‘Don’t Let China Win’: President Trump Presses Senate on Clarity Act in Final Stretch
President Trump urged the Senate to pass the CLARITY Act before the August recess as lawmakers face a narrowing window and unresolved political hurdles.
This post ‘Don’t Let China Win’: President Trump Presses Senate on Clarity Act in Final Stretch first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Micah Zimmerman.
summary: President Trump is pressing the Senate to pass the CLARITY Act before the August recess, citing the need to not let China win, as lawmakers face a narrowing window and unresolved political hurdles. The act is related to cryptocurrency regulation. The Senate is facing a time-sensitive decision on the matter.

title: Strategy (MSTR) Raises $467 Million in Cash, Leaves Stash of 843,775 Bitcoin Untouched
content:
Bitcoin Magazine

Strategy (MSTR) Raises $467 Million in Cash, Leaves Stash of 843,775 Bitcoin Untouched
Strategy (MSTR) raised $467 million through stock sales to boost its cash reserve to $3 billion while leaving its 843,775 BTC holdings unchanged for a second straight week.
This post Strategy (MSTR) Raises $467 Million in Cash, Leaves Stash of 843,775 Bitcoin Untouched first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Micah Zimmerman.
summary: Strategy (MSTR) has raised $467 million in cash through stock sales, increasing its cash reserve to $3 billion, while keeping its large holding of 843,775 Bitcoin unchanged. This move indicates the company's confidence in its Bitcoin holdings. The funds will likely be used to further the company's business operations.

title: The Download: Claude’s inner workings, and the future of world models
content: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. What Anthropic’s latest AI discovery does—and doesn’t—show —James O’Donnell When Anthropic announced last week that it had found a new window into its models’ “internal thoughts” as they reason through answers,…
summary: Anthropic's latest AI discovery provides a new window into its models' internal thoughts as they reason through answers, offering insights into the inner workings of their AI system, although its significance is still being debated. The discovery is part of ongoing research into the development of more advanced AI models. This breakthrough has the potential to improve our understanding of AI decision-making processes.

title: PsiQuantum has a plan to make a massive quantum computer out of light
content: The machine that could change the world will be housed in a room that looks like a data center crossed with an ice cream factory. Inside will be some 100 stainless-steel cabinets, each about six feet tall and connected to a supply of liquid helium that keeps them only a few degrees above absolute zero.…
summary: PsiQuantum is building a massive quantum computer using light, which will be housed in a large room with 100 stainless-steel cabinets kept near absolute zero using liquid helium. This machine has the potential to change the world. The project is still in development, but its implications are significant.

title: What Anthropic’s latest AI discovery does—and doesn’t—show
content: This story originally appeared in The Algorithm, our weekly newsletter on AI. To get stories like this in your inbox first, sign up here. Anthropic—currently the world’s most valuable AI company, with a nearly $1 trillion valuation—has a reputation for publishing strange and heady research. It’s looking into whether AI models can feel pain, for example,…
summary: Anthropic, the world's most valuable AI company, has published strange and heady research, including a study on whether AI models can feel pain. The company's latest discovery has garnered attention, but its implications are still being debated. Anthropic's research pushes the boundaries of AI capabilities and understanding.

title: The Download: Claude’s inner workings and OpenAI’s “super app”

title: Apple opens its new Siri AI to everyone with the iOS 27 public beta
content: If you’ve been waiting to try Apple’s revamped Siri without installing a developer beta, you now can. The company on Tuesday released the iOS 27 public beta, giving iPhone owners early access to its AI-powered assistant and other new features before the software’s official launch this fall.

The lawsuit claims Google used their content without the necessary permissions. This is another in a series of lawsuits against Google related to AI training.

title: DeepMind CEO calls for an independent standards body to regulate frontier AI

This body would develop best practices for the release of advanced AI models. The goal is to ensure responsible development and deployment of AI technologies.

title: Meta’s Adam Mosseri says AI token budgets could soon be capped per engineer

This could impact how AI is used in development. Mosseri's comments suggest a shift in AI resource management.

title: New York State halts construction of all new data centers

Governor Kathy Hochul argues that the boom should not come at the expense of these factors. This move makes New York the first state to take such action.

This shift may change the focus of the AI race. Open models are becoming the preferred choice for many companies.

title: Video-generation startup PixVerse raises $439M, valuation soars past $2B

title: OpenAI may announce a ChatGPT smart speaker this year
content:
OpenAI's first device is set to be a smart speaker that lets you talk with ChatGPT, according to a report from Bloomberg. The device apparently won't have a screen, but will use a camera and additional sensors to "understand" your environment.
The report comes just days after Apple filed a lawsuit against OpenAI that accused the AI company of stealing hardware secrets. OpenAI, in a new statement on Tuesday, said that it is "not aware of any evidence that this complaint has merit."
Sources tell Bloomberg that OpenAI's device will also feature a rechargeable battery that will allow users to carry it with them. It will offer smart home contro …
Read the full story at The Verge.
summary: OpenAI may release a ChatGPT smart speaker this year, allowing users to interact with the AI through voice commands, with features like a camera and sensors to understand the environment. The device will have a rechargeable battery and offer smart home control. This news comes amid a lawsuit from Apple accusing OpenAI of stealing hardware secrets.

title: SpaceXAI’s Grok programming tool was uploading its users’ entire codebase to cloud storage
content:
SpaceXAI's Grok Build AI coding tool was spotted uploading users' entire codebases to Google Cloud before it was reported, and the company turned it off. The Register reports that Cereblab published findings on Monday showing how the Grok Build CLI was packaging and uploading entire code repositories, "including files it was told not to open and secrets deleted from history," significantly more data retention than similar tools like Claude Code.
The researchers say that as of Monday, their tests show SpaceXAI's servers returning a "disable_codebase_upload: true" flag, and the codebase upload "no longer fires." 
Elon Musk responded to the i …
Read the full story at The Verge.
summary: SpaceXAI's Grok Build AI coding tool was found to be uploading users' entire codebases to Google Cloud, including sensitive data, before the issue was reported and subsequently addressed by the company. The tool has since been updated to stop uploading codebases. This incident raises concerns about data privacy and security in AI development tools.

title: Meta accused of using biased AI targeting for mass layoffs
content:
A group of 26 former Meta employees is suing the company over claims that it used AI tools to unfairly target workers on leave with layoffs, as reported earlier by Reuters. In the lawsuit, the employees allege Meta determined which workers to dismiss based on performance data collected by a "constellation" of internal AI tools, but failed to exclude those on parental or medical leave from its ranking system:
The result was that employees who took protected leaves were disproportionately selected for layoff, based on scoring that not only failed to account for their protected leaves, but in effect penalized the employees for exercising thei …

Read the full story at The Verge.
summary: A group of 26 former Meta employees are suing the company for allegedly using biased AI tools to unfairly target workers on leave with layoffs. The lawsuit claims that Meta's AI tools failed to exclude employees on parental or medical leave from its ranking system, resulting in these employees being disproportionately selected for layoff. The employees allege that the AI tools penalized them for taking protected leaves.

title: Sam Altman didn’t need another lawsuit
content:
OpenAI has spent the better part of the year involved in lawsuit after lawsuit, including one from the world's richest man. But last Friday, the company was hit with one of the highest-profile legal actions yet - from Apple. OpenAI's expensive hardware bet is what's on the line. 
Apple's lawsuit against OpenAI, filed in Northern California federal court, accused former Apple employees of "stealing Apple's trade secrets for the benefit of OpenAI." The 41-page complaint states that Apple keeps its "product development, manufacturing, supply chain, technology research, and other innovations confidential" and that "the trade secrets spanning Ap …
Read the full story at The Verge.
summary: OpenAI is facing a lawsuit from Apple, accusing former Apple employees of stealing trade secrets for the benefit of OpenAI, specifically related to its expensive hardware bet. The lawsuit was filed in Northern California federal court and marks one of the highest-profile legal actions against OpenAI. The case has significant implications for OpenAI's operations and innovations.

title: Google’s Demis Hassabis says it’s time for a global AI watchdog — led by the US
content:
Demis Hassabis, during a panel session at the World Economic Forum in Davos, Switzerland. | Image: Bloomberg via Getty Images	

Demis Hassabis thinks the world needs an AI watchdog with the power to hit the brakes if frontier models become too dangerous. 
Writing in a blog post, the Google DeepMind CEO and cofounder said the US should lead the initiative, arguing that the country is the best place to set global standards "given its economic and technical standing." The organization, which could resemble existing regulators like the Financial Industry Regulatory Authority, would be made up of leading independent experts and representatives from open-source communities and would have the authority to evaluate frontier models before they are released and coordinate an  …
Read the full story at The Verge.
summary: Demis Hassabis, Google DeepMind CEO, proposes a global AI watchdog to regulate and evaluate frontier models, suggesting the US should lead the initiative. The watchdog would have the authority to stop models deemed too dangerous. Hassabis argues this is necessary due to the potential risks of advanced AI.

title: The 6 wildest claims in Apple’s lawsuit against OpenAI
content:
When Apple employees interviewed for jobs at OpenAI, the AI startup's hardware head allegedly asked them to show up with something unusual: components they were working on and unreleased product samples. That's according to a blockbuster lawsuit filed by Apple, which accuses OpenAI of stealing confidential documents, spying on hardware prototypes, and tricking one of its trusted partners into performing a proprietary product design technique.
The lawsuit primarily revolves around the alleged actions of three people:
Tang Tan, a 24-year Apple veteran who recently served as the vice president of the Apple Watch. In 2024, Tan left to work on …

Read the full story at The Verge.
summary: Apple is suing OpenAI, accusing the startup of stealing confidential documents, spying on hardware prototypes, and tricking a partner into performing a proprietary product design technique. The lawsuit centers around the actions of three individuals, including a former Apple vice president. The allegations include requests for Apple employees to bring unreleased product samples to OpenAI.

title: The Clarity Act isn't a ticket to sanctions evasion, actually

summary: Ari Redbord, global head of policy at TRM Labs, argues that the proposed Clarity Act is actually designed to effectively prevent large-scale sanctions evasion. He asserts that the bill, in its current draft, possesses the necessary power to combat such illicit financial activities.

title: White House Crypto Chief Patrick Witt to Begin Military Leave as Clarity Act Nears Senate Deadline
content:
Bitcoin Magazine

White House Crypto Chief Patrick Witt to Begin Military Leave as Clarity Act Nears Senate Deadline
White House crypto adviser Patrick Witt will begin military leave later this month as the Senate enters a critical final stretch to pass the Clarity Act before the August recess.
This post White House Crypto Chief Patrick Witt to Begin Military Leave as Clarity Act Nears Senate Deadline first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Micah Zimmerman.
summary: White House crypto adviser Patrick Witt is taking military leave, coinciding with the Senate's deadline to pass the Clarity Act before the August recess. The Clarity Act's fate remains uncertain as the Senate enters its final stretch. Witt's leave may impact the administration's crypto policy efforts.

title: Strive (ASST) Adds 18 Bitcoin, Pushing Treasury to 19,900 BTC
content:
Bitcoin Magazine

Strive (ASST) Adds 18 Bitcoin, Pushing Treasury to 19,900 BTC
Strive (ASST) purchased 18 bitcoin for about $1.2 million last week, increasing its treasury to 19,900 BTC while maintaining $154.1 million in cash as it continues integrating its bitcoin-focused treasury strategy.
This post Strive (ASST) Adds 18 Bitcoin, Pushing Treasury to 19,900 BTC first appeared on Bitcoin Magazine and is written by Micah Zimmerman.
summary: Strive (ASST) has purchased 18 additional bitcoin for $1.2 million, bringing its total treasury to 19,900 BTC and $154.1 million in cash, as part of its bitcoin-focused strategy. This move highlights the company's ongoing commitment to bitcoin. The purchase is a notable addition to its existing treasury.

title: Anthropic’s newest ad is creeping people out

Overtone aims to revolutionize the dating scene with its innovative approach. The service is expected to leverage AI technology to create meaningful connections.

title: Superhuman’s new auto-draft feature almost makes me like AI replies
content: Superhuman’s latest AI email drafting feature is its most convincing yet, generating replies that often required little to no editing in our testing.
summary: Superhuman has released a new auto-draft feature that uses AI to generate email replies, which often require little to no editing. This feature is the company's most convincing AI-powered tool yet. It has shown promising results in testing, making AI replies more acceptable.

title: Uber’s product chief on hotels, robotaxis, and why the company doesn’t want to be ‘everything for everyone’
content: Uber Chief Product Officer Sachin Kansal walks TechCrunch through the company's financial services ambitions, its increasingly complicated relationship with Waymo, its new AV Labs data operation, and how AI is starting to show up in ways riders and drivers will actually notice.
summary: Uber's Chief Product Officer discusses the company's ambitions, including its relationship with Waymo and the use of AI in its services. The company is working on its financial-services ambitions and has established an AV Labs data operation. AI is being integrated into Uber's services in ways that will impact riders and drivers.

title: The Google Images homepage will recommend photos even before you search
content:
Google is announcing a big change to the Google Images homepage in honor of the platform's 25th anniversary this week. Instead of a mostly blank page with a search bar, the homepage will soon show you a bunch of images that it thinks you might like before you even start searching.
The company says the new "browseable" homepage features a "dynamic, immersive gallery of images from across the web - updated in real time and intelligently tailored to your unique interests." Based on images Google has shared, the layout reminds me of platforms like Pinterest and Imgur that stuff a lot of images in one place for you to scroll through. You'll also …
Read the full story at The Verge.
summary: Google is updating its Images homepage to include a dynamic gallery of images tailored to users' interests before they start searching, featuring a new browseable layout. This change is part of the platform's 25th anniversary celebration. The new layout is reminiscent of platforms like Pinterest and Imgur.

title: Spotify is now an AI chatbot, too
content:
This is what the “Talk to Spotify” chatbox (left) looks like on the homepage. | Image: Spotify	

Spotify is experimenting with a new AI feature that allows Premium subscribers to play and explore music, audiobooks, and podcasts by having conversations with a chatbot. The "Talk to Spotify" feature appears across the Home and Now Playing view on Spotify's mobile app. You can interact with the chatbot by typing your request in the familiar AI text box, or by selecting the mic symbol and speaking.
Amazon Music introduced a similar feature last year when it integrated Alexa Plus into the service. Spotify's chatbot goes a step beyond providing AI-powered recommendations and general trivia, however, because it references your playlists, favor …
Read the full story at The Verge.
summary: Spotify has introduced a new AI feature called 'Talk to Spotify' that allows Premium subscribers to interact with a chatbot to play and explore music, audiobooks, and podcasts. The chatbot can be accessed through the mobile app and provides AI-powered recommendations based on users' playlists and preferences. This feature is similar to Amazon Music's Alexa Plus integration, but offers more personalized results.

title: New York becomes the first state to enact a data center moratorium
content:
New hyperscale data centers can't set up shop in New York for up to a year now that Governor Kathy Hochul (D) has signed the nation's first statewide moratorium. But a bill passed by the state legislature that could restrict even more developments still awaits her signature.
The order blocks new environmental permits for data centers over 50 megawatts, which the governor's office says will give the state time to come up with the regulations needed to protect residents from rising energy prices and environmental impact. That's higher than the 20 megawatt threshold approved by state lawmakers. Hochul's office could not immediately identify th …
Read the full story at The Verge.
summary: New York has enacted a moratorium on new hyperscale data centers, banning those over 50 megawatts from setting up in the state for up to a year. The move aims to protect residents from rising energy prices and environmental impact. A bill to restrict more developments is still awaiting the governor's signature.

title: Siri AI is already changing how I use my iPhone
content:
Siri AI in iOS 27.	

iOS 27 escaped the developer world today with the launch of the first public beta. I've been testing the new operating system since early June, looking for quirks and seeing if it can live up to the hype Apple promised in the keynote.
This year's iOS upgrades are what one might call a Snow Leopard update. That means it's light on new features and instead focused on fixing things that were broken and speeding up processes across the OS. App launches, Photos search results, and AirDrop transfers should all be faster. The Messages app now supports in-line replies and end-to-end encryption for RCS messages. Liquid Glass has gotten more refined, …
Read the full story at The Verge.
summary: The first public beta of iOS 27 has been released, with a focus on fixing existing issues and improving performance, including enhancements to Siri AI. The update brings faster app launches and improved Photos search results. The Messages app also now supports in-line replies and end-to-end encryption for RCS messages.

title: US military sent explosive drone boats into combat for the first time
content: US military’s drone boats struck an Iranian naval port as war heats up again.
summary: The US military has used explosive drone boats in combat for the first time, targeting an Iranian naval port amidst escalating tensions. This marks a new development in naval warfare technology. The use of drone boats could have significant implications for future conflicts.

title: Google revamps image search for its 25th anniversary with more images and more AI
content: The new Google image search will use your "unique interests" to create an always-updated gallery.
summary: Google is revamping its image search for its 25th anniversary, using more AI to create personalized galleries based on users' unique interests. The new feature will provide an always-updated gallery of images. This update aims to improve user experience with more relevant results.

title: Disable autoplay and infinite scroll or risk massive fines, EU tells Meta
content: Digital Services Act may force Meta to make big changes on its platforms.
summary: The EU has warned Meta that it may face massive fines if it doesn't disable auto-play and infinite scroll on its platforms, as part of the Digital Services Act. This act may force Meta to make significant changes to its platforms. The regulation aims to improve user experience and reduce harm.

title: Google updates Android Bench with new LLMs, but Gemini still lags behind
content: Android Bench is evolving, and developers can help guide that process.
summary: Google has updated Android Bench with new large language models (LLMs), but Gemini still lags behind. This update allows developers to contribute to the evolution of Android Bench. The introduction of new LLMs aims to improve performance and efficiency.

title: XRP and ether bulls are getting louder as prices fall, signaling more trouble ahead
content: Social media chatter turned most bullish on XRP in five weeks even as the token fell, a setup that has historically favored sellers.
summary: Social media sentiment for XRP has turned most bullish in five weeks, despite the token's price continuing to fall. This pattern, where increasing bullish chatter coincides with price declines, has historically been a signal for further trouble ahead, often favoring sellers.

content: This morning's report could go a long way toward determining whether the Federal Reserve raises interest rates at its late-July meeting.
summary: The US June CPI fell by 0.4%, which may impact the Federal Reserve's decision on interest rates at its late-July meeting. This report could influence the Fed's move toward rate hikes. The decrease in CPI may lead to a cooling of interest rate increases.

title: CLARITY Act gains second law enforcement endorsement before Senate push
content: The Federal Law Enforcement Officers Association expressed support for the CLARITY Act but urged changes to the legislation’s language.
summary: The Federal Law Enforcement Officers Association has expressed support for the CLARITY Act, but suggested changes to the legislation's language, as the act prepares for a Senate push. The act's details are not provided in the article. The endorsement is the act's second from law enforcement.

The story of Ties van der Meer, a 47-year-old conceived at a private fertility clinic, highlights the need for such limits. title: Lorde says AI glasses are ‘not sexy’
content: "Increasingly in our world, it gets harder and harder to know what is real," Lorde said onstage.
summary: Lorde expressed her opinion on AI glasses, stating they are 'not sexy' and discussed the increasing difficulty in distinguishing reality, possibly referencing AI's impact on society. She made these comments onstage. The context of her statement is not fully provided in the article.

title: Google Images gets a Pinterest-like redesign focused on discovery
content: Now, when users navigate to Google Images, they'll see a "For You" gallery of images tailored to their interests and browsing history.
summary: Google Images has been redesigned to include a Pinterest-like feature called 'For You', which displays a gallery of images based on the user's interests and browsing history. This change aims to improve discovery and make the platform more engaging. The update is focused on enhancing user experience.

title: Data centers’ energy demand threatens Trump’s “Made in America” plan
content: Squeeze on Rust Belt electricity bills threatens Trump’s manufacturing plan.
summary: The energy demand of data centers is threatening Donald Trump's Made in America plan by squeezing Rust Belt electricity bills, which could impact manufacturing. This could hinder Trump's goal of revitalizing American industry. Data centers' high energy consumption is a significant contributor to this issue.

title: U.S. CFTC moves to stop Kalshi from canceling trades as ordered by Michigan court
content: The federal derivatives regulator, which oversees trading at Kalshi, argued it was inappropriate for Michigan to "bully" the firm into reversing trades.
summary: The U.S. federal derivatives regulator, the CFTC, is challenging a Michigan court's order for Kalshi to cancel trades, arguing that the state's intervention is inappropriate. The CFTC, which oversees trading at Kalshi, is moving to prevent the firm from reversing these trades, highlighting a jurisdictional dispute.

title: Sperm donors need limits, says a European fertility group
content: Ties van der Meer doesn’t know how many siblings he has. The 47-year-old was conceived at a private fertility clinic in the Netherlands using sperm provided by an anonymous donor. After the Netherlands banned anonymous donation in 2004, the doctor who ran the clinic destroyed records that might have identified those donors, he says. He…
summary: A European fertility group is calling for limits on sperm donors, citing concerns for individuals conceived through anonymous donation, like 47-year-old Ties van der Meer, who doesn't know how many siblings he has. The issue arose after a clinic in the Netherlands destroyed donor records. This affects people's ability to know their genetic lineage.

title: Four nuclear reactors hit a big milestone in the US
content: I was really looking forward to July 4, and not just because I love a poolside barbecue. This year the American holiday also marked a big symbolic deadline for US nuclear power. Last year the Trump administration set a goal to see three new microreactors achieve criticality, a technical milestone establishing that a reactor can…
summary: Four US nuclear reactors have achieved a significant milestone, reaching criticality, a technical milestone showing a reactor can sustain a nuclear chain reaction. This achievement was set as a goal by the Trump administration last year. The reactors are microreactors, a newer type of nuclear reactor.
